META_DESCRIPTION: A clean, native desktop download manager for yt-dlp. Download videos, audio, playlists, subtitles, and metadata with a simple app for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
OG_DESCRIPTION: Download videos, audio, playlists, subtitles, and metadata with a clean desktop app for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
TWITTER_DESCRIPTION: Download videos, audio, playlists, subtitles, and metadata with a clean desktop app for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
HERO_TITLE: The easy desktop face of yt-dlp.
HERO_SUBTITLE: Paste a URL, preview the media, choose a format, and let KoalaPull handle yt-dlp and ffmpeg without the terminal.
FEATURES_SUBTITLE: Everything you need to download media with less friction and more control.
FEATURE_1_DESC: KoalaPull downloads yt-dlp and ffmpeg into your app data folder, so setup is one click and done.
FEATURE_2_DESC: See the title, uploader, thumbnail, duration, and available formats before you start a job.
FEATURE_3_DESC: Pick best quality, audio-only, or a custom container and subtitle combo that fits your needs.
FEATURE_4_DESC: Add multiple downloads, keep them moving, and run up to 10 jobs in parallel with live progress.
FEATURE_5_DESC: Handle full playlists, then review past downloads with timestamps, sizes, and speed stats.
FEATURE_6_DESC: Use light or dark mode, check for updates, and work across hundreds of supported sites.
MOCKUP_SUBTITLE: A static clickdummy of the KoalaPull desktop app.
MOCK_DOWNLOADS_FETCH_HELP: Grab title, formats, duration, and playlist count before you queue.
MOCK_SETTINGS_THEME_DESC: Light and dark modes follow your saved preference.
MOCK_SETTINGS_LANGUAGE_DESC: Switch the app UI between the supported locales.
MOCK_SETTINGS_OUTPUT_DESC: Choose where finished downloads are written.
MOCK_SETTINGS_CONCURRENCY_DESC: Set how many jobs can run at once.
MOCK_SETTINGS_AUTOPASTE_DESC: Optional clipboard detection for supported links.
MOCK_HELP_STEP_1: Paste a video or playlist URL into Downloads.
MOCK_HELP_STEP_2: Fetch metadata and check the preview.
MOCK_HELP_STEP_3: Choose a preset or custom format.
MOCK_HELP_STEP_4: Add it to the queue and watch progress.
MOCK_HELP_SITES_TEXT: YouTube, Vimeo, Twitch, TikTok, SoundCloud, Bandcamp, and hundreds more through yt-dlp.
HOWTO_DESC: A simple path from copied URL to finished file.
HOWTO_STEP_1_TEXT: Copy a video or playlist link from your browser and drop it into KoalaPull.
HOWTO_STEP_2_TEXT: Inspect title, thumbnail, duration, uploader, and available formats before downloading.
HOWTO_STEP_3_TEXT: Use the recommended preset or switch to audio-only and custom options when you need them.
HOWTO_STEP_4_TEXT: Queue one or many jobs, then let KoalaPull run up to 10 downloads in parallel.
HOWTO_STEP_5_TEXT: Watch the progress, then open the output folder when the download is finished.
FAQ_SUBTITLE: Answers to the questions people usually ask first.
FAQ_Q1: Is KoalaPull free and open source?
FAQ_A1: Yes. KoalaPull is open source under the MIT License and free to use.
FAQ_Q2: Do I need yt-dlp or ffmpeg installed first?
FAQ_A2: No. KoalaPull downloads the required tools into your app data folder on first launch.
FAQ_A3: KoalaPull uses yt-dlp, so it can work with hundreds of supported sites, including YouTube, Vimeo, Twitch, SoundCloud, and more.
FAQ_Q4: Can it handle playlists and subtitles?
FAQ_A4: Yes. Playlist downloads, subtitle options, and metadata previews are built into the app.
FAQ_A5: It is local-first and privacy-friendly. No telemetry, no external CDNs, and no account required.
FAQ_Q6: Why use KoalaPull instead of the terminal?
FAQ_A6: KoalaPull keeps the power of yt-dlp but gives you buttons, queues, history, and settings in a native desktop app.
CTA_TITLE: Ready to pull your next download?
CTA_TEXT: Grab the latest release or inspect the code on GitHub. KoalaPull is built for people who want yt-dlp power without terminal friction.
FOOTER_MIT: Open source under the MIT License.
